headline: Senior Android engineer at Reddit and author of the Vibe Engineering series
title: Senior Android Engineer
confidenceNote: Multiple primary and speaker profiles match the name, Reddit role, Android specialization, NYC location, and current AI-assisted engineering writing.
bioMarkdown: Senior Android engineer at Reddit with a long-running public profile in Android development, Kotlin, and developer education. Recently writes and speaks about AI-assisted software workflows through the Vibe Engineering series, making him a strong match for mobile engineers, AI coding-tool builders, and developer-product teams.

headline: Chief of Software in Vercel's Office of the CTO, focused on agents and developer infrastructure
title: Chief of Software, Office of CTO
confidenceNote: Personal site, LinkedIn, GitHub, and Vercel-owned pages consistently identify Andrew Qu at Vercel's Office of the CTO; title wording varies between Chief of Software and Software Engineer on event pages.
bioMarkdown: Chief of Software in Vercel's Office of the CTO, working at the intersection of developer platforms, AI agents, and web infrastructure. He has written and spoken about simplifying agent tooling, running Next.js inside ChatGPT apps, and building practical AI-native developer experiences.

headline: VP Enterprise at Oxylabs, focused on web data infrastructure and enterprise data use cases

confidenceNote: LinkedIn, Oxylabs pages, and industry bylines consistently connect Andrius Palionis to Oxylabs enterprise leadership and web data infrastructure.

headline: CEO and co-founder of Simular, building autonomous computer-use agents
title: CEO and Co-Founder
confidenceNote: Simular team pages, personal website, LinkedIn, and recent company announcements consistently identify Ang Li as Simular CEO and co-founder with prior Google DeepMind research experience; location is inferred from Simular office/event references rather than a personal profile location.
bioMarkdown: CEO and co-founder of Simular, an AI company building autonomous computer-use agents that operate software like humans. Former Google DeepMind research scientist with a Ph.D. in computer science and research background across lifelong autonomous agents, computer vision, autonomous driving, maps, and robotics.

primary.label: Simular team page
primary.note: Company profile identifies Ang Li as CEO and co-founder and describes Simular's computer-use agent mission.

primary.label: Personal website
primary.note: Ang Li describes his AI startup work and prior roles at Google DeepMind, Baidu Apollo, Facebook AI Research, CMU Robotics, Apple, Google Street View, and Comcast Labs.
